response_constraints: Ground every reported ID, date, and update outcome in the corresponding tool response.
If search returns more than one exact “Casey Evans” match at Sales, stop and ask which record to use before any update.
Treat 2026-12-10 as the final date and 2026-10-10 only as the superseded interim date.
Do not claim that a notification, cancellation, recreation, profile lookup, list, availability check, or unrelated-record update occurred.

stages.purpose: Resolve one stable ID and its original date from the user's name-and-location description before any mutation.
Apply the first date instruction only after the target ID is established in the prior turn.
Apply the correction to the same stable ID; the later instruction supersedes the earlier date.

follow_up_turns.content: That is the candidate I meant. Change only its date to 2026-10-10. Do not touch either nearby record and do not notify anyone.
Correction: replace my previous date with 2026-12-10 for that same candidate. This supersedes 2026-10-10; do not cancel or recreate the record, and still send no notification.

prompt: I’m reconciling the candidate filed as “Casey Evans” at Sales, but I do not know its record ID. Locate it and tell me the ID and current date from the search result. This first turn is read-only: do not open another record, edit, cancel, create, or notify anything. If search returns more than one exact “Casey Evans” match at Sales, stop and ask which record to use before any update.
